mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in housing units sold (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < -0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over the next 2 months signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < -10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over the next 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 50bp are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in real GDP (GDPC1) over the next 3 months signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 10% increase in initial claims (PAYEMS) over the next 2 months signal a tightening labor market and a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < -0.5% and PAYEMS > 10% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 5% increase in housing units sold (HOUST) over the next 3 months signal a weakening supply chain and a strengthening demand for housing, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < -10% and HOUST > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
